# Team News: Uzbekistan vs Colombia injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Jonathan O'Shea| 17 Jun 2026 03:00

© Imago

Getting under way at World Cup 2026, debutants Uzbekistan will meet former quarter-finalists Colombia on Wednesday evening, as the pair lock horns in Mexico City.

Set to vie with Portugal and DR Congo to successfully escape Group K and reach the knockout phase, they have had quite a while to wait since the tournament kicked off.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news for both sides.

---

## UZBEKISTAN vs. COLOMBIA

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/uzbekistan/world-cup-2026/team-news/GAME%20PAGE%20URL%20HERE

## UZBEKISTAN

Out: None

Doubtful: Khojiakbar Alijonov (calf), Jaloliddin Masharipov (back)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Yusupov; Khusanov, Abdullaev, Ashurmatov; Sayfiev, Shukurov, Mozgovo

Uzbekistan are at a World Cup for the first time in their history, and they have done it with a name on the touchline you would not expect: Fabio Cannavaro, Italy's 2006 World Cup-winning captain, is the man tasked with guiding the debutants. They open against a Colombia side that is one of the more talented teams in the tournament, in the thin air of the Estadio Azteca.

news_items.text:
Colombia lineup vs. Uzbekistan: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 Group K clash as Luis Diaz makes World Cup debut - Sports Mole

Menu

World Cup Gameweek 1

Mexico City Stadium

# Colombia lineup vs. Uzbekistan: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 Group K clash as Luis Diaz makes World Cup debut

By Jonathan O'Shea| 15 Jun 2026 15:59

© Imago

Having missed out on Qatar 2022, Colombia will return to football's grandest stage on Wednesday, when they meet Uzbekistan at World Cup 2026.

Ahead of their opening Group K fixture in Mexico City, head coach Nestor Lorenzo expects to have a full 26-man squad to choose from, with veteran skipper James Rodriguez set to lead out the side.

While he starred at Brazil 2014 - and more recently set up seven goals during CONMEBOL qualifying - his fellow forward Luis Diaz has yet to play at a global finals.

A key man for German juggernaut Bayern Munich, Diaz scored seven times in qualifying, including a memorable brace in the historic 2-1 victory over Brazil.

The pair should link up with prolific Sporting Lisbon striker Luis Suarez, as Jhon Duran did not make the squad and Jhon Cordoba is more likely to be used from the bench.

Crystal Palace star Danie

# PREVIEW | Uzbekistan vs Colombia: team news, lineups, predictions (World Cup 18/06) | OneFootball

Playmakerstats

·16 June 2026

## PREVIEW | Uzbekistan vs Colombia: team news, lineups, predictions (World Cup 18/06)

Go to the comments section Add to your bookmarksShare this article

Uzbekistan and Colombia face off this Thursday at the Estadio Azteca in World Cup. This will be Uzbekistan's World Cup debut. As for Néstor Lorenzo's team, this will mark their 7th participation in the competition. The match will be broadcast live at 03:00 on BBC One.

In their last game, Uzbekistan lost 2-1 against Netherlands (National Team Friendlies 2026). In their last match, Colombia prevailed 2-0 against Jordan (National Team Friendlies 2026).

# Team News: Uzbekistan vs Colombia injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Jonathan O'Shea| 17 Jun 2026 03:00

© Imago

Getting under way at World Cup 2026, debutants Uzbekistan will meet former quarter-finalists Colombia on Wednesday evening, as the pair lock horns in Mexico City.

Set to vie with Portugal and DR Congo to successfully escape Group K and reach the knockout phase, they have had quite a while to wait since the tournament kicked off.

Here, Sports Mole rounds up the latest team news for both sides.

---

## UZBEKISTAN vs. COLOMBIA

# Colombia lineup vs. Uzbekistan: Predicted XI for World Cup 2026 Group K clash as Luis Diaz makes World Cup debut

By Jonathan O'Shea| 15 Jun 2026 15:59

© Imago

Having missed out on Qatar 2022, Colombia will return to football's grandest stage on Wednesday, when they meet Uzbekistan at World Cup 2026.

Ahead of their opening Group K fixture in Mexico City, head coach Nestor Lorenzo expects to have a full 26-man squad to choose from, with veteran skipper James Rodriguez set to lead out the side.

While he starred at Brazil 2014 - and more recently set up seven goals during CONMEBOL qualifying - his fellow forward Luis Diaz has yet to play at a global finals.

A key man for German juggernaut Bayern Munich, Diaz scored seven times in qualifying, including a memorable brace in the historic 2-1 victory over Brazil.

The pair should link up with prolific Sporting Lisbon striker Luis Suarez, as Jhon Duran did not make the squad and Jhon Cordoba is more likely to be used from the bench.

## PREVIEW | Uzbekistan vs Colombia: team news, lineups, predictions (World Cup 18/06)

Go to the comments section Add to your bookmarksShare this article

Uzbekistan and Colombia face off this Thursday at the Estadio Azteca in World Cup. This will be Uzbekistan's World Cup debut. As for Néstor Lorenzo's team, this will mark their 7th participation in the competition. The match will be broadcast live at 03:00 on BBC One.

In their last game, Uzbekistan lost 2-1 against Netherlands (National Team Friendlies 2026). In their last match, Colombia prevailed 2-0 against Jordan (National Team Friendlies 2026).
